zone_summary: Insights v2.0.6 replaces dateRange with a strict DateTimeOffset start/end contract on /summary, /trends, and /errors/top; centralizes 60-day/future/order validation and UTC day bucketing; and makes summary previous-period math culture-invariant.

behavioral_changes.summary: The /summary, /trends, and /errors/top actions no longer expose a dateRange query parameter; they now accept nullable DateTimeOffset startTime/endTime inputs, and the XML contract text was updated to describe the stricter v2.0.6 caller rules.
All three v2.0.6 insights endpoints now route through ResolveStrictDateRange, which applies missing-bound defaults independently and emits UTC yyyy-MM-dd strings for downstream queries.
Strict date comparison now happens at full DateTimeOffset instant precision before day bucketing, so same-day windows remain valid when endTime is later than startTime.
The shared strict resolver enforces the new 60-day envelope by rejecting starts older than 60 days and also carrying an explicit >60-day span guard.
The shared strict resolver now rejects endTime values that are in the future.
The shared strict resolver now requires endTime to be strictly greater than startTime; equal or earlier end values are rejected.
GetSummaryAsync now parses and formats its previous-period boundary with InvariantCulture so prevStartDate is locale-independent.

grounding_evidence.reason: Introduces the new 60-day bound used by the strict v2.0.6 date contract.

grounding_evidence.excerpt:
/// <param name="startTime">Optional start time (ISO-8601 date-time). Must be within the last 60 days and ≤ endTime. Defaults to now − 7 days.</param>
/// <param name="endTime">Optional end time (ISO-8601 date-time). Must be > startTime and ≤ now. Window must not exceed 60 days. Defaults to now.</param>
grounding_evidence.reason: Contract-bearing XML comments redefine the /summary query contract around startTime/endTime and remove the old dateRange description.

grounding_evidence.excerpt:
[FromQuery] DateTimeOffset? startTime = null,
[FromQuery] DateTimeOffset? endTime = null,
grounding_evidence.reason: The /summary action signature now exposes only nullable DateTimeOffset bounds and no dateRange parameter.
The /summary request pipeline now uses the shared strict resolver instead of the old dateRange-aware path.
Summary previous-period math now parses and formats with InvariantCulture, changing locale-sensitive behavior.

grounding_evidence.excerpt:
/// <param name="startTime">Optional start time (ISO-8601 date-time). Must be within the last 60 days and ≤ endTime. Defaults to now − 7 days.</param>
/// <param name="endTime">Optional end time (ISO-8601 date-time). Must be > startTime and ≤ now. Window must not exceed 60 days. Defaults to now.</param>
grounding_evidence.reason: Contract-bearing XML comments apply the same stricter caller contract to /trends.

grounding_evidence.excerpt:
[FromQuery] DateTimeOffset? startTime = null,
[FromQuery] DateTimeOffset? endTime = null,
grounding_evidence.reason: The /trends action signature now exposes only nullable DateTimeOffset bounds and no dateRange parameter.
The /trends request pipeline now uses the shared strict resolver.

grounding_evidence.reason: The /errors/top action signature now exposes only nullable DateTimeOffset bounds and no dateRange parameter.
The /errors/top request pipeline now uses the shared strict resolver.
This is a contract-bearing summary comment that explicitly narrows the allowed input domain and documents same-day/day-bucket behavior for all three endpoints.
Implements independent defaults for omitted start and end bounds.
Adds the 400 path for starts older than the v2.0.6 60-day floor.
Adds the 400 path for future endTime values.
Adds the strict-order 400 path covering both equal and reversed bounds.
Encodes the explicit 'window must not exceed 60 days' branch of the new date contract.
Normalizes explicit/defaulted bounds into UTC yyyy-MM-dd strings, which is the observable query-service input shape.
Adds an explicit contract test that locks in removal of the dateRange parameter from the three v2.0.6 endpoints.

scenario_sketches.title: v2.0.6 insights actions expose only DateTimeOffset start/end bounds and no dateRange
scenario_sketches.rationale: Reflect over GetSummaryAsync, GetTrendsAsync, and GetTopErrorsAsync (or generated API metadata) to assert there is no dateRange query parameter, startTime/endTime are nullable DateTimeOffset query args, and the published contract text matches the stricter v2.0.6 caller shape.

scenario_sketches.title: Missing date bounds default independently to now - 7d and now across summary/trends/top-errors
scenario_sketches.rationale: Exercise three partitions: neither bound supplied, start-only supplied, and end-only supplied. Verify the shared strict resolver fills each missing side independently and the three endpoints all route through that behavior.

scenario_sketches.title: Explicit DateTimeOffset bounds are normalized to UTC yyyy-MM-dd before querying
scenario_sketches.rationale: Pass explicit timestamped start/end values and assert the downstream query-service call receives UTC day strings, not local-time or raw timestamp text.

scenario_sketches.title: Same-day timestamp windows are accepted when endTime is later than startTime
scenario_sketches.rationale: Use two instants on the same UTC day to prove the accept/reject decision is based on full DateTimeOffset precision before day truncation; a same-day later end should still return OK.

scenario_sketches.title: Requests beyond the 60-day date envelope return 400
scenario_sketches.rationale: Probe the 60-day boundary and just beyond it to preserve the v2.0.6 envelope. This covers the observable contract that too-old requests are rejected and anchors the explicit >60-day-span branch as part of the same 60-day limit.

scenario_sketches.title: Future endTime is rejected on the strict insights endpoints
scenario_sketches.rationale: Set endTime just after UtcNow and verify the shared strict resolver returns BadRequest before any query execution.

scenario_sketches.title: endTime must be strictly after startTime
scenario_sketches.rationale: Cover both reversed bounds and equal instants, since the new rule is a strict end > start invariant rather than end >= start.

scenario_sketches.title: Summary previous-period start remains culture-invariant
scenario_sketches.rationale: Run GetSummaryAsync under a non-invariant CurrentCulture and assert prevStartDate still uses yyyy-MM-dd and correct period math, proving the new InvariantCulture parse/format calls are required.
